page: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is a virtual machine instance within the High Frequency Compute family. It features 6 vCPUs mapped to 3 physical Intel x86_64 cores with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 448 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across standard compute tasks, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1670.29 and a multi-core score of 9438.87. The instance demonstrates strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 33.56 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth exhibits notable bottlenecks; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 674.29 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths drop to weak and poor categories at 26.11 GB/sec and 13.1 GB/sec respectively. Additionally, database operations and static web serving throughput are relatively weak, with the latter measuring 6.1 GB/sec.

Given the shared CPU allocation and the observed memory bandwidth limitations, this instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight web serving, and moderate computational tasks rather than memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference. It delivers average performance for small-scale LLM prompt processing but performs poorly on larger 7B models. The inclusion of fast local NVMe SSD storage and a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides reasonable resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for development environments, build systems, and intermediate application hosting.
description: The Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is a High Frequency Compute instance featuring 6 shared vCPUs on an Intel x86_64 architecture with 3 physical cores, 24.0 GB of RAM, and 448 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor delivers average single-core and multi-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, the instance exhibits weak to poor uncached memory bandwidth and below-average database operation speeds. While it handles small-scale language model inference and general-purpose tasks adequately, it is less suited for memory-intensive applications or large-scale model execution. It represents a qualitatively cost-effective option for general-purpose workloads, development environments, and moderate web serving where local NVMe storage is beneficial.
og_description: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is a High Frequency Compute instance with 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 448 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced general-purpose performance with fast local storage.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining balanced memory capacity with fast local NVMe storage for general-purpose and development workloads.

24.0 GB of system memory and 448 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 33.56 GB/sec
